what_it_does: Acceptance test for the I5 'annotate vs operate' routing seam: it verifies that clicking a ui:// design element without a consequential verb attaches a comment (annotate face), while clicking a run:// live instance or a consequential verb at a governed address routes through the operate face and is gated by the I4 governance tier (bare/untiered addresses act immediately; CONFIRM/LOCKED tiers surface for approval).
observations: Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 and uses a temporary FsStore and Suite to exercise routing.
Creates a small graph (constant -> uppercase) to have a live node instance.
Checks that Suite exposes route_click, act, and annotate as the I5/I2/I4/I6 composition points.
Verifies ui://chrome/inbox with no verb routes to annotate, persists a comment, and produces no action or surfaced item.
Verifies a bare verb='run' click routes to operate, acts immediately (did=='run'), and moves real state.
Verifies run://{graph}/u routes to operate and acts via the verb-class fallback.
Injects a tiered ui:// address into UI_REGISTRY (extras tier='source_data') rather than editing addresses.json.
Verifies a consequential verb at a CONFIRM/LOCKED-tier address surfaces for approval (did=='surfaced_for_approval'), does not execute the graph, and writes no annotation.
Verifies 'show' at an untiered ui:// element routes to operate and acts immediately as a read-only camera drive.
Includes blur guards: annotate(run://...) raises; route_click(ui://..., no text, no verb) raises; operate routes never add annotations.
standards: The test explicitly treats scheme as a routing hint, not the safety gate; the real gate is capability plus the I4 governance tier.
Tiered addresses are injected through the INSTANCE registry (UI_REGISTRY extras), respecting F4's read-only addresses.json territory.
Discriminating assertions are designed to fail naive implementations that blur annotate and operate.
conventions: Heavy docstring maps every behavior to implementation-guide sections (I5, I6, I4, U1, F4, CONTRACT.2).
Uses a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper.
Uses tempfile + shutil.rmtree for isolated store cleanup.
Monkeypatches suite.UI_REGISTRY to inject a tiered row for testing.
concerns: Catches broad Exception in blur-guard checks, so any unrelated error would also make the test pass.
Some assertions are tautological or check internal state shapes that are not contractually guaranteed (e.g., r['action']['routed_posture'], suite._tier_for_address).
Relies on internal methods/attributes such as UI_REGISTRY, _tier_for_address, list_surfaced, and inbox.is_approved.
The check that annotations_at(ui_elem) == [a for a in suite.annotations_at(ui_elem)] is always true and does not actually verify no new annotation was added.
notes: A contract-level acceptance test that the annotate and operate faces stay structurally separate and that governance tier, not URL scheme, gates mutating commands.
questions: What exact signature and return shape does Suite.route_click promise?
How does _tier_for_address map an injected UI_REGISTRY row to a governance tier?
What does the act() method return and how is 'routed_posture' computed?
Is UI_REGISTRY intended to be mutated directly by tests, or is there a supported fixture API?
How does annotate() detect and reject run:// addresses?
What side effects does COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 trigger in governance/inbox code?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it exercises annotate-vs-operate routing and tier-gated operation surfacing.
apparent_themes: Routing by capability and governance tier rather than string scheme.
Strict separation between annotation (design commentary) and operation (live graph mutation).
Preservation of immediate/auto read-only verbs (show) and untiered run paths (U1).
Safety through surfacing consequential commands for approval at CONFIRM/LOCKED tiers.
intention_signals: To make it impossible for a UI/design click to silently execute a consequential command, and impossible for a live operation click to silently become a comment, by routing each through its own face and reusing the existing I4 governance tier unchanged.
novelty: It explicitly rejects 'scheme alone gates mutation' and instead tests that the same ui:// scheme can either annotate (no verb) or operate (immediate verb), while a tiered ui:// address with a consequential verb proposes rather than executes.
contribution: A concrete acceptance test that encodes the I5 routing contract, the blur guards, and the preservation of U1 immediate-run and read-only show behavior, so a fused system can keep these semantics as a regression spec.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test validating that ui:// design clicks annotate, run:///consequential-verb clicks operate through the I4 governance tier, and the two faces never blur.
intention_for_embedding: To lock down the annotate-vs-operate routing seam and prove that governance tier, not URL scheme, gates mutating commands.

symbols.does: Test helper that asserts a condition and increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) is a module-level assertion helper used throughout the acceptance test: it asserts cond with a FAIL label, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints an 'ok' line. It effectively turns the script into a lightweight inline test runner.
